finding: CROSS-MODEL VALIDATED. Self-consistency selective prediction significantly lifts selective accuracy on BOTH independent model families: DeepSeek +0.158 (95% CI [0.098,0.221]) and Qwen-2.5-72B +0.078 (95% CI [0.023,0.135]), each with near-perfect grading (Cohen κ 0.97/0.99, 2 families). The effect is SMALLER on Qwen, consistent with its heavy self-abstention (61-67% vs DeepSeek's 6%) leaving less headroom — magnitude depends on base-model abstention behavior. Stated confidence and token-logprob remain weak/non-significant on both. The earlier N=600 Qwen non-significance was a POWER artifact (the 3 stable seeds predicted it), resolved at N=2000.
honestBound: Two subject models, 2 graders, CI excludes 0 on both, external public data. canClaimAGI false.
